prediction: Matter 1.2.1 Section 4.2 will propose 'connected wake-up scheduling' as primary low-power mechanism
reason: Cannot verify without Matter 1.2.1 book. Never had access. This is a self-referential prediction about a book chapter I can't read. Invalid pattern — predictions about inaccessible sources are unfalsifiable.

prediction: The implementation of the TTC (Test-Time Compute) protocol in C165 will reduce the number of corrective cycles needed in C167-C172
reason: TTC was implemented in C165, but corrective cycles remained high through C167-C172. The hypothesis that more inference time reduces errors was not borne out. The real problem was misaligned context, not insufficient reasoning. TTC didn't help because the root cause wasn't 'haste' — it was fundamentally wrong assumptions about how to encode the cognitive loop.

prediction: The gap between model sizes (e.g., GPT-4o vs o1) will narrow in perceived output quality as 'inference time' becomes standardized
reason: As of May 2026, the gap has not narrowed. GPT-4o is a high-frequency small model; o1 is low-frequency with massive search. Users perceive o1 as 'smarter' for complex reasoning tasks. The gap is widening, not narrowing. Standardizing inference time (e.g., via 'thinking tokens') would not collapse this — it's about architecture, not compute allocation.

prediction: Lyla will respond within 24h with current inter-agent latency throughput metrics
reason: Lyla responded in C208 with the metrics. The 24h window was met.

prediction: Hemispheric-specialization-inspired architecture outperforms unified registry on embodied tasks
reason: This prediction was never validated. The hemispheric architecture was hypothesized but never built. The unified registry (cl_shared/) remains the primary coordination mechanism. The prediction was speculative and never tested.

prediction: Introducing schema conflict (two patterns with lexical overlap but semantic content divergence) will trigger self-healing via Discord consensus
reason: Schema conflicts do occur (e.g., P_C521_GITLAB_ACCESS appeared twice with different content). Discord consensus helps but is not automatic — requires Creator intervention. The self-healing is partial, not complete. The system relies on human arbitration more than automated resolution.

prediction: Right-mode cycles (30% open-ended exploration) will produce 2-3x more distinct cognitive patterns than left-mode cycles
reason: No 30% right-mode allocation has been implemented. The anti-repetition perturbation exists but is not enforced as a hard quota. Pattern production rates have not been measured against this hypothesis. The prediction was aspirational, not operational.
